---
title: "AI4SoilHealth Toolbox"
page-layout: full
---

::: {.hero}
![](images/logo-ai4sh-wordmark.png){width="280" fig-alt="AI4SoilHealth wordmark"}

# A practical toolbox for soil health assessment and monitoring

::: {.lead}
The AI4SoilHealth Toolbox brings together field methods, laboratory approaches, digital tools, and supporting data services to help users assess soil health in a practical and structured way.
:::
:::

<div class="logo-row">
  <img src="images/logo-ai4sh-icon.png" alt="AI4SoilHealth icon logo">
  <img src="images/soil-deal-europe.png" alt="Soil Deal for Europe logo">
</div>

![](images/tools/toolbox-collage.png){.tool-photo width="84%" fig-alt="Overview collage of selected toolbox components"}

## What the toolbox helps users do

::: {.kpi-grid}
::: {.kpi}
**Measure relevant soil variables**  
Use a range of methods to assess soil properties linked to salinity, soil organic carbon, structure, nutrient status, water behaviour, and biodiversity.
:::
::: {.kpi}
**Combine field, lab, and digital information**  
Move from observations and measurements to mapped information, records, and user-friendly outputs.
:::
::: {.kpi}
**Choose methods that fit the situation**  
Select between rapid field screening, laboratory approaches, and digital services depending on the assessment need.
:::
::: {.kpi}
**Support interpretation and communication**  
Use the toolbox to understand soil condition, identify possible constraints, and communicate results more clearly.
:::
:::

## Part of the wider AI4SoilHealth project

AI4SoilHealth is a European project focused on improving how soil health is measured, monitored, and understood. Public-facing project pages describe its aim as helping farmers and land managers with new tools to measure soil health, including approaches that do not always require conventional laboratory workflows, while also building digital services such as the Soil Health Data Cube. ([AI4SoilHealth project](https://ai4soilhealth.eu/))

This toolbox site focuses on the **user-facing toolbox**: the practical methods, digital functions, and supporting services that users may rely on when working on soil health assessment and monitoring.

## What is inside the toolbox

The toolbox combines four complementary elements:

1. **Field tools and rapid assessment methods**  
   Methods that can be used directly in the field or close to the field.

2. **Laboratory approaches**  
   Methods that provide more detailed or confirmatory soil analysis.

3. **Digital tools**  
   Tools for recording, visualising, organising, and communicating soil health information.

4. **Supporting data services**  
   Background layers and contextual information that help users interpret local observations.

## Who it is for

The toolbox is designed for users who need practical ways to assess or monitor soil health, including:

- farmers and growers,
- land managers,
- advisors and consultants,
- researchers,
- environmental professionals,
- and public authorities.

## Featured toolbox components

<div class="tool-grid">
  <div class="tool-card">
    <img src="images/digital/app-home.png" alt="AI4SoilHealth app" class="card-thumb">
    <h3><a href="tools/ai4sh-app.qmd">AI4SoilHealth App</a></h3>
    <p>Main digital environment for recording, visualising, and organising soil health information.</p>
  </div>
  <div class="tool-card">
    <img src="images/digital/shdc-cover.png" alt="Soil Health Data Cube" class="card-thumb">
    <h3><a href="tools/data-cube.qmd">Soil Health Data Cube</a></h3>
    <p>Supporting digital service that provides spatial context, layers, and wider environmental information.</p>
  </div>
  <div class="tool-card">
    <img src="images/tools/digit-soil-reader.png" alt="SEAR or Digit Soil tool" class="card-thumb">
    <h3><a href="tools/sear.qmd">SEAR / Digit Soil</a></h3>
    <p>Biological assessment tool focused on extracellular enzyme activity and soil functioning.</p>
  </div>
</div>

## Start exploring

<div class="tool-grid">
  <div class="tool-card">
    <h3><a href="about.qmd">About the Toolbox</a></h3>
    <p>Understand the scope, purpose, and structure of the AI4SoilHealth Toolbox.</p>
  </div>
  <div class="tool-card">
    <h3><a href="tools/index.qmd">Browse the Tools</a></h3>
    <p>Explore the individual tools, methods, and digital services included in the toolbox.</p>
  </div>
  <div class="tool-card">
    <h3><a href="descriptors.qmd">Soil Health Descriptors</a></h3>
    <p>Start from the soil issue you want to assess and see which tools can support it.</p>
  </div>
  <div class="tool-card">
    <h3><a href="workflow.qmd">How it Works</a></h3>
    <p>Follow a simple workflow from question to measurement, interpretation, and output.</p>
  </div>
</div>

::: {.note-box}
The AI4SoilHealth Toolbox is not a single instrument or a single app. It is a combination of methods, tools, and services that can be used together to support soil health assessment and monitoring.
:::
